opp.ais_why:
• Direct Travel's intro centered on contract renewal reminders, missed-payment risk and Salesforce integration - classic CLM and AP pain with no AI vocabulary from the prospect.
• Zycus probed for Teams/Slack collaboration and AI use cases but received no engagement on Merlin, ANA or autonomous negotiation themes.
• Single 24-minute discovery, post-ATPI acquisition chaos, AIS field is null - sparse evidence supports Resistant.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell (buyer speaker)
SF Description (inferred owner: Partner Engagement + Finance)
metrics.narrative: Michael Kell articulated clear operational pain on the 31 Mar 2026 discovery call: invoicing delays create open liabilities to partners, risk of double/missed payments in a decentralized post-acquisition environment (37 acquisitions in 8 years, ATPI added 100 offices globally). He stated 'we're at risk getting missed payments, double payments' and described a reactive invoicing process where customers sit on invoices for 90-120 days while Direct Travel carries open partner liabilities. The financial exposure is real but NOT quantified—no dollar figures for missed/double payments, no cycle-time baselines, no savings target. Kell noted the process shifted to his oversight only in the last 2-3 months, so historical impact data may not be compiled. The pain owner is clear (Kell, Partner Engagement + the unidentified CFO who oversees finance). To advance, we need to co-build a quantified business case: dollar value of payment errors, invoice cycle time vs target, ROI from automation.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell active participation
Avoma action items: Kell to consult IT, consider roadshow
SF tasks 22 Jun: Kell + Guylaine Cliche (engagement breadth)
No evidence of EB access, internal advocacy, or competitive deflection
champion.narrative: Michael Kell (Director Partner Engagement) is a developing champion. Evidence of engagement: he actively participated in the 31 Mar discovery call, articulated detailed pain and use case (CLM + eInvoicing for partner upsells, Salesforce integration), and committed to two internal actions—consult IT on sourcing processes and consider attending the Zycus Chicago roadshow (early Apr, now past). He has been referenced in recent tasks (22 Jun LastActivityDate) alongside Guylaine Cliche, suggesting ongoing two-way dialogue. However, champion strength is DEVELOPING, not strong, because: (1) Kell explicitly stated 'very little of my role is involved directly in procurement'—he is an influencer/user, not a budget holder or exec sponsor; (2) no evidence of advocacy (no introductions to EB/CFO, no internal selling, no competitive deflection); (3) access to power is unknown—he mentioned the CFO and IT but has not facilitated intros; (4) engagement is recent but passive (we invited him to the roadshow; he said he'd 'look at my calendar'). To strengthen: secure his active advocacy (ask him to bring CFO or VP Finance into next call), confirm he completed the IT consultation, convert his pain into a written business case he can champion internally.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026 (zero competitive discussion)
SF Competitors__c null, no competitive fields populated
No Tasks/Next Steps referencing rivals
competition.narrative: Competition is unknown. The 31 Mar discovery call included NO mention of other vendors, incumbent CLM/invoicing tools being evaluated, or competitive timeline pressure. Michael Kell described the current state as manual/offline ('quotes done in email', 'no Salesforce visibility', 'invoices sent by account managers'), implying a do-nothing / status-quo alternative rather than an active bake-off. He noted Direct Travel is 'reengineering' Salesforce with a 'new Salesforce team,' which could mean they are evaluating Salesforce-native CLM (e.g. Conga, Docusign CLM) or invoicing add-ons, but he did not name any. Competitors__c in Salesforce is null. No Next Step or Task references a rival. The competitive landscape is completely dark. At this stage (Qualified, 87 days in) that is a red flag—either we are sole-sourced (unlikely for a $100k cross-functional deal) or we have not asked. The next call MUST surface: Are you evaluating other CLM/invoicing vendors? What tools does IT/Finance prefer? Is there an incumbent contract management or AP automation system? If we are in a bake-off and don't know it, we are flying blind.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell (buyer speaker)
Avoma notes: 'no reliable system for invoicing customers', 'at risk getting missed payments, double payments', 'very complex process to inherit'
identify_pain.narrative: Michael Kell, Director Partner Engagement, owns a clear, urgent operational and financial pain articulated on the 31 Mar 2026 call. Pain: Direct Travel's invoicing and contract management for third-party partner upsells (duty-of-care platforms, tech partners) is decentralized, offline (email-based quotes), and reactive—account managers invoice customers AFTER receiving partner invoices, leading to 90-120 day customer payment delays while Direct Travel carries open liabilities to partners. Kell stated: 'There is currently no reliable system for invoicing customers, leading to delays in sending invoices and potential liabilities to partners' and 'We're at risk getting missed payments, double payments.' The root cause is post-acquisition complexity: 37 acquisitions in 8 years, ATPI acquisition added 100 global offices, processes remain decentralized. Kell noted 'it's been a very complex process to inherit...in what used to be and still is to some degree pretty decentralized environment.' He took over the financial component only in the last 2-3 months after discovering gaps (no contract amendments, no Salesforce visibility, invoices sent ad hoc). Pain owner: Kell (Partner Engagement) and the broader Finance/AR-AP teams. Compelling event: recent centralization push under new CFO/exec team. This is confirmed, urgent, and owned.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026 (no paper discussion)
SF fields: Current_Contract_Expiration__c null, no partner/SI references
paper_process.narrative: Paper process (MSA, SOW, legal, InfoSec, procurement channel) is completely unknown. The 31 Mar call focused on use case and pain; no contracting, security, or implementation scoping discussion occurred. Michael Kell mentioned Direct Travel is 'reengineering' Salesforce and has a 'new Salesforce team,' implying a complex IT landscape and likely InfoSec/integration review requirements for a CLM + eInvoicing + SFDC integration deal, but he did not describe the approval or vendor onboarding process. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c forcing function. No SI/partner involvement mentioned. At 127 days to close, we need to surface the paper path (legal review SLA, InfoSec questionnaire, procurement VP sign-off, implementation/integration scoping) in the next discovery call or we risk a late-stage surprise that blows the close date.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unidentified. The 31 Mar call was discovery-level with Michael Kell (Director Partner Engagement), who described his use case (partner upsell CLM + eInvoicing) but made no mention of budget authority, approval process, or executive sponsorship. He noted the CFO (recently moved to lead ATPI acquisition integration) and a 'new Salesforce team' reengineering the platform, implying cross-functional budget decisions, but no EB was named or engaged. The deal has zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles, so no formal EB/exec sponsor is mapped in Salesforce. Kell's comment 'I'll talk to our IT department' suggests he is an influencer/user, not the budget holder. The EB is likely the CFO (Kristine O'Shea per account contacts) or an SVP Finance/IT given the cross-functional scope (CLM + invoicing + Salesforce integration). No pricing or commercial conversation has occurred. This is a critical gap for a $100k deal at 127 days to close.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026 (no process discussion)
SF Next_Step__c (internal Zycus actions, no buyer milestones)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unknown. The 31 Mar call was discovery-only; no discussion of approval chain, timeline gates, or evaluation steps occurred. Michael Kell said he would 'talk to the IT department' to understand their sourcing processes and invited them to a potential follow-up, suggesting a multi-department evaluation is anticipated but not yet structured. The deal has zero buyer-side contact roles, so the committee composition is unmapped. No mention of legal review, InfoSec, procurement approval, or executive sign-off. The Next Step (Zycus internal review, SE discovery call, roadshow invite) implies early-stage relationship-building, not a formal vendor evaluation. For a $100k cross-functional deal (CLM + eInvoicing + Salesforce integration), a structured process with IT, Finance, Legal, and exec sponsor is expected but not yet defined. We need to surface and map the process in the next call.

sources: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell functional requirements
No RFP/RFI, no multi-stakeholder validation recorded
decision_criteria.narrative: Michael Kell outlined four explicit functional requirements on the 31 Mar call: (1) automated 90-day renewal alerts to finance for proactive customer billing, (2) centralized quote + contract amendment capture (currently done offline via email), (3) Salesforce integration to record partner upsells as Opportunities and track multi-year terms, (4) biweekly supplier meeting note-taking within the CLM. He also expressed interest in supplier risk monitoring ('risk factors as far as monitoring partners would be of interest') though his portfolio is small (~10 partners). These are USER criteria from one influencer; no formal RFP, no evaluation scorecard, no cross-functional stakeholder input (IT, Finance, Legal have not weighed in). Kell acknowledged 'very little of my role is involved directly in procurement'—the broader org's criteria (IT's laptop/hardware sourcing, Marketing's RFP needs) are unknown. Decision criteria are partially known from one lens but not validated across the buying committee.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI appetite is unknown. AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, and AIS_Why__c are all null in Salesforce, so no formal AI Hungry/Curious/Resistant tier is recorded. The 31 Mar discovery call included no buyer-initiated AI questions or AI-driven use case exploration. Michael Kell's articulated pain (automated invoicing, liability management, centralized processes) is operationally automatable and a natural Merlin fit, but he did not frame it in AI terms or express AI curiosity. Direct Travel is reengineering Salesforce and has a 'new Salesforce team,' suggesting openness to modern tech, but AI readiness is unconfirmed. Infer AI Curious (operational automation appetite, modern tech posture) but not AI Hungry (no explicit AI agenda). The next call should test AI appetite: frame Merlin's autonomous negotiation, invoice exception AI, and risk monitoring as the forcing function for Kell's pain and gauge his response.

items.title: Vice President, Technology Solutions

items.detail: Economic Buyer (CFO Kristine O'Shea or SVP Finance Brett Fearrin) is unidentified and unengaged at 87 days in Qualified, 127 days to close. Michael Kell is an influencer, not a budget holder. For a $100k cross-functional deal, exec sponsorship and budget validation are critical by this stage. Risk: late-stage EB engagement surfaces budget constraints, competing priorities, or approval delays that blow the close date.

Single-threaded through Michael Kell, who is not a decision-maker. Zero OpportunityContactRoles. IT (Rodney Foster, Brianna Conway) and Finance (Brett Fearrin, Kristine O'Shea) stakeholders are unmapped and unengaged. Kell committed to consult IT but no follow-up confirmed. Risk: if Kell disengages, moves roles, or loses internal priority, the deal ghosts. A single-threaded deal at 127 days to close is at high stall/ghost risk.

Competition is completely unknown (Competitors__c null, zero call discussion). Direct Travel is reengineering Salesforce, which could bring Salesforce-native CLM (Conga, Docusign) or AP automation alternatives into play, but no competitive intelligence has been gathered. Risk: we are positioning in a vacuum; a rival may already be framing the evaluation, and we have no wedge or competitive deflection strategy. At 87 days in Qualified, this is a blind spot.

Decision process and evaluation timeline are unknown. No discussion of approval chain, RFP/RFI, vendor selection gates, or legal/InfoSec review on the 31 Mar call. Michael Kell's commitment to consult IT and potential roadshow attendance (early Apr, now past) are the only near-term milestones, and neither is a formal evaluation step. At 127 days to close, a structured process with IT, Finance, Legal should be defined by now. Risk: we are assuming a timeline that does not match the buyer's actual approval process, leading to a late-stage slip.

Salesforce integration is a critical requirement (Michael Kell: 'Integration with Salesforce is critical to capture contract amendments, quotes and upsells'). Direct Travel is reengineering Salesforce with a 'new Salesforce team,' implying complex integration requirements, IT approval, and potential InfoSec/data governance review. No IT stakeholder (Rodney Foster VP IT, Brianna Conway VP Tech Solutions) has engaged to validate integration feasibility, API requirements, or implementation timeline. Risk: late-stage IT veto or integration complexity that extends implementation and blows the close date.

Budget authority and $100k approval process are unconfirmed. Michael Kell has not discussed budget, pricing, or ROI expectations. Economic Buyer is unidentified. Risk: we assume $100k is approved, but late-stage EB engagement surfaces budget constraints, competing capex priorities (ATPI acquisition integration, Salesforce reengineering), or a lower threshold requiring exec approval we did not plan for.

champion_strength.summary: Michael Kell (Director Partner Engagement) is a developing champion with active engagement but limited power and no evidence of internal advocacy. He articulated clear pain and use case on the 31 Mar call and has maintained recent contact (22 Jun task), but he explicitly stated 'very little of my role is involved directly in procurement,' signaling he is an influencer/user, not a budget holder. He has not facilitated introductions to the Economic Buyer (CFO/SVP Finance) or IT stakeholders, and his commitment to consult IT and attend the roadshow was passive ('I'll look at my calendar'). Champion strength is DEVELOPING because engagement is real but advocacy, EB access, and internal selling are unproven. To strengthen: ask Kell to bring CFO or VP Finance into the next SE discovery call, confirm he completed the IT consultation, and co-build a quantified business case he can champion to exec sponsors.

confidence_signals.summary: Mixed signals. Positive: Michael Kell engaged substantively on 31 Mar discovery call with detailed pain articulation and functional requirements; recent activity (22 Jun LastActivityDate, 4 days ago) confirms live engagement; Kell committed to internal IT consultation and roadshow consideration. Negative: 87 days in Qualified with no stage movement since creation; zero buyer-side OpportunityContactRoles (single-threaded); Economic Buyer unidentified; decision process, competitive landscape, and paper process completely unknown; no cross-functional stakeholder engagement (IT, Finance, Legal unengaged); Kell's passive commitment ('I'll look at my calendar' re roadshow) and explicit statement he is not a procurement decision-maker. The deal is LIVE but thin—engaged through one influencer with no exec sponsorship, no multi-threading, and no formal evaluation process defined. At 127 days to close, these gaps are forecast-defensibility risks.

north_star_verdict.math: 127 days to close (close 31 Oct 2026). Created 31 Mar 2026, now 87 days old. Stage=Qualified, so average expected time-in-stage for enterprise deals ~60-90 days before Shortlisted; current 87 days in Qualified is at the upper end but not alarming given the discovery call occurred on day 1 (31 Mar) and multiple near-term actions are queued. LastActivityDate 22 Jun 2026 (4 days ago) confirms live engagement. No stage slips recorded. Close date is achievable if the deal advances to Shortlisted within ~30 days and clears Vendor Selected / Negotiation gates by late Aug/early Sep, leaving ~60 days for contracting. Forecast category Pipeline is appropriate at this stage.

Michael Kell (Director Partner Engagement) engaged substantively on the call, articulated specific use case (CLM + eInvoicing for partner upsells, Salesforce integration), committed to internal IT consultation

127 days to close allows ~30 days discovery/Shortlist, ~60 days Vendor Selected/Negotiation, ~30 days contracting
north_star_verdict.headline: EARLY-stage deal progressing with clear pain, engaged champion (Kell), and recent activity (4 days ago). 87 days in Qualified is upper-bound normal; next actions queued. Key blind spot: competitive landscape completely unknown—next call must surface if other vendors are evaluating.

best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage win-strategy levers for this deal: (1) Michael Kell is an influencer, not the budget holder—map and engage the Economic Buyer (likely CFO Kristine O'Shea or SVP Finance Brett Fearrin) within the next 14 days to validate budget, timeline, and exec sponsorship, or risk a late-stage surprise; (2) competition is completely dark at 87 days in Qualified—surface the competitive landscape (other CLM/invoicing vendors, Salesforce-native alternatives, incumbent systems) in the next call to position our wedge before a rival frames the evaluation; (3) the deal is single-threaded through Michael Kell, who explicitly stated he is not a procurement decision-maker—multi-thread to IT (Rodney Foster, VP IT; Brianna Conway, VP Tech Solutions) and Finance (Brett Fearrin, CFO Kristine O'Shea) to de-risk champion flight and build cross-functional consensus.

competitive_position.summary: Competition is completely unknown. No vendors named in any source (Salesforce Competitors__c null, zero mentions in the 31 Mar discovery call, no Task/Next Step references). Michael Kell described the current state as manual/offline (email-based quotes, no CLM, reactive invoicing by account managers), suggesting the primary threat is DO-NOTHING / status quo rather than an active rival. However, Direct Travel is reengineering Salesforce with a new team, which could bring Salesforce-native CLM or AP automation alternatives into play (e.g. Conga, Docusign CLM, Coupa Invoicing), but Kell did not confirm. At 87 days in Qualified with zero competitive intelligence, this is a blind spot. The next call must surface whether other vendors are being evaluated, what IT/Finance prefer, and if an incumbent system exists.

items.grounding_quote: Consult the IT department to determine current sourcing processes and procurement tool usage. I'll find out how are they doing their sourcing.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI/Merlin positioning is absent. Products in scope include Merlin (AI negotiation agent), but the 31 Mar discovery call included zero AI discussion. Michael McCarthy (Zycus rep) walked through the full source-to-pay suite and mentioned 'AI is built into our entire technology stack' and 'autonomous negotiation' in passing, but did not tie Merlin to Michael Kell's articulated pain (invoicing automation, liability management, partner risk monitoring). Kell's use case (automated 90-day alerts, Salesforce integration, centralized quote capture) is a strong Merlin fit (Merlin can drive proactive renewal workflows, invoice exception-flagging via AP Smartdesk, and risk monitoring), but this wedge was never positioned. AIS fields are null, so no AI appetite signal from Salesforce. At 87 days in Qualified with Merlin in the product scope, the AI story must be framed in the next SE call or it becomes a checkbox, not a differentiator.

items.quote: Automated invoicing workflows should issue 90-day renewal alerts to finance for proactive customer billing, ensuring timely partner payments and liability management.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell
items.criterion: Automated 90-day renewal alerts to finance for proactive invoicing

items.quote: Integration with Salesforce is critical to capture contract amendments, quotes and upsells of third-party platforms into opportunities and record multi-year terms.
items.criterion: Salesforce integration to capture partner upsells, contract amendments, quotes, and multi-year terms in Opportunities

items.quote: Centralize quotes and contract amendments for service upgrades currently handled via email to ensure proper invoicing and liability management.
items.criterion: Centralized quote and contract amendment capture (currently email-based, offline)

items.quote: I have meetings with my suppliers or my partners a minimum every two weeks...if there's some type of a note taking feature that would be really interesting.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell
items.criterion: Biweekly supplier meeting note-taking feature within CLM

items.quote: Interest in monitoring partner risk factors.
items.criterion: Supplier risk monitoring (geopolitical, financial, operational risk for ~10 partners)

items.quote: Very little of my role is involved directly in procurement. I'm sure there's more traditional procurement type applications for this within our company.
items.source: Avoma discovery call 31 Mar 2026, Michael Kell
items.criterion: Cross-functional procurement needs (IT hardware/laptop sourcing, Marketing RFP workflows) - unvalidated

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Strong product-fit signal on the articulated use case (CLM + eInvoicing + Salesforce integration for partner upsell management), but scope is narrow (one influencer's lens) and cross-functional fit is unvalidated. Michael Kell's requirements align well with Zycus iContract (CLM with renewal alerts, note-taking), eInvoicing (automated AP/AR workflows, 90-day alerts), and Merlin (though AI positioning was not discussed). However, Kell stated 'very little of my role is involved directly in procurement'—the broader org's needs (IT's hardware/laptop sourcing, Marketing's RFP workflows, Finance's AP automation standards) are unknown, and those stakeholders have not engaged. Risk: we are fitting to one user's narrow problem while the economic buyer and IT may have different priorities or preferred vendors.

hard.ais_why:
• Direct Travel's intro centered on contract renewal reminders, missed-payment risk and Salesforce integration - classic CLM and AP pain with no AI vocabulary from the prospect.
• Zycus probed for Teams/Slack collaboration and AI use cases but received no engagement on Merlin, ANA or autonomous negotiation themes.
• Single 24-minute discovery, post-ATPI acquisition chaos, AIS field is null - sparse evidence supports Resistant.
hard.next_step:
• Zycus to review use case internally with solution consultants
  • Zycus to revert with tailored solution approach
  • Prospect to gather insights on sourcing processes
  • Invite prospect to attend upcoming Zycus Chicago roadshow
  • Loop in SE for a discovery call

pulse.summary: Live: last verified Salesforce activity 4 day(s) ago (2026-06-22).

rep_outreach.note: rep reached out, awaiting buyer reply

deltas.subject: COMPETITION IS DARK—SURFACE IT BEFORE A RIVAL FRAMES THE EVAL. Zero competitive intelligence at 87 days in Qualified (Competitors__c null, no call discussion, no Task references). Direct Travel is reengineering Salesforce, which could bring Salesforce-native CLM (Conga, Docusign) or AP automation (Coupa Invoicing) into play. The next call must ask: Are you evaluating other vendors? What does IT/Finance prefer? Is there an incumbent CLM or invoicing system? If we are in a bake-off and don't know it, we cannot position our AI/Merlin differentiation or address competitive objections. Make this a top-3 discovery question for the SE call.

SINGLE-THREADED THROUGH A NON-DECISION-MAKER—MULTI-THREAD TO POWER. Michael Kell is the sole engaged contact (0 OpportunityContactRoles); he acknowledged he is not a procurement decision-maker and committed to consult IT. The deal MUST multi-thread to: (1) IT (Rodney Foster VP IT, Brianna Conway VP Tech Solutions) to validate Salesforce integration requirements and procurement tool preferences; (2) Finance (Brett Fearrin SVP Finance, CFO Kristine O'Shea) to validate invoicing pain, AP/AR process, and budget. Use the SE discovery call to request a cross-functional stakeholder session (Kell + IT + Finance) and add confirmed participants as OpportunityContactRoles. A single-threaded deal through an influencer at 127 days to close is at high risk of ghost/stall if Kell disengages.
